# RustyNES v2.0.4 — "Harbor" ("Slipway")

**The Android release candidate.** The fourth release of the v2.0.x "Harbor" mobile-finalization train stages the release-candidate scaffolding a maintainer needs to upload the Android app onto a Play Console testing track. The cycle-accurate emulation core is **unchanged and byte-identical to v2.0.3** — **AccuracyCoin 141/141 (100.00%)**, nestest 0-diff, the deterministic `#![no_std]` chip stack untouched — so **no accuracy, save-state, or determinism number moves in this release**. This is a host / Android-only cut, and the `foss` (F-Droid / GitHub-sideload) flavor stays **behaviour-identical**.

No store submission happens here (that is the future v2.1.0 joint Google Play + Apple App Store + AltStore PAL + F-Droid launch); v2.0.4 brings the Android build to the point a maintainer can drive the on-device closeout.

---

## The core is unchanged (141/141)

Nothing in the emulation core changed. The 2-cycle-ALE PPU fetch model promoted to default in v2.0.3 (ADR 0030) is the substrate here too; AccuracyCoin holds **141/141 (100.00%, RAM-authoritative)**, nestest is 0-diff, and the 60-ROM commercial byte-identity oracle is unmoved. If you only care about emulation accuracy, v2.0.4 is v2.0.3 with a bumped version number.

## Host-side RC scaffolding (what actually landed)

- **Release signing wired with a graceful debug-signing fallback.** The `release` build type now always resolves a signing config: the real **upload keystore** (a gitignored `keystore.properties` file or the `RUSTYNES_UPLOAD_*` env vars — a maintainer secret injected in CI / locally, **never committed**) when present, otherwise a **fallback to the debug signing config** rather than leaving the release APK unsigned. A keyless CI or local `assemble{Foss,Play}Release` therefore still produces an installable RC artifact for smoke testing (debug-signed, and so **never shippable** — Play App Signing rejects the debug key). A real store upload uses the upload key.
- **Crash / ANR hardening.** A debug-only **StrictMode** installer (`DebugStrictMode`) surfaces the two most common ANR / jank sources during development — main-thread disk / network I/O (thread policy) and leaked `Closeable`s / SQLite cursors / registered receivers (VM policy). It is **log-only** (never `penaltyDeath`) and **guarded on `BuildConfig.DEBUG`**, so a release / RC / store build constructs no policy and carries zero runtime cost or behavioural change. It is installed as the first statement of `MainActivity.onCreate` so launch-path violations are caught too, and is shared by `foss` + `play`. It complements the existing opt-in, privacy-first `CrashReporter` (local crash logs, uploads nothing).
- **Fastlane / Play Console listing metadata (version-controlled).** A `fastlane/metadata/android/{en-US,es-ES}/` tree — `title.txt`, `short_description.txt`, `full_description.txt`, and `changelogs/20004.txt` — stages the store listing as tracked source a maintainer uploads via fastlane / the Play Console. Copy is accurate to the app (a cycle-accurate NES emulator, bring-your-own-ROMs, open source) and flags the RC / testing-track status.
- **R8 / ProGuard final hardening review.** The release keep set was re-audited for the RC and confirmed **complete** — the UniFFI (`rustynes_mobile` plus monetization) plus JNA surface, the `NativeRenderer` JNI seam, and the play-flavor Google / AppLovin / RevenueCat SDKs are all covered; the v2.0.4 host additions need no new keeps. No existing keep was loosened; the audit outcome is recorded in `proguard-rules.pro`.
- **Version bump.** Workspace `2.0.3 → 2.0.4`; Android `versionCode 20003 → 20004`, `versionName "2.0.3" → "2.0.4"`.

## Verification

- **Core unchanged / byte-identical to v2.0.3** — AccuracyCoin **141/141**, nestest 0-diff (not re-run for this host-only cut; the chip stack is untouched).
- `cargo check --workspace` green after the version bump + `Cargo.lock` regen (version-only lock diff); `cargo fmt --all --check` clean.
- Android **`:app:compileFossDebugKotlin` + `:app:compilePlayDebugKotlin` both BUILD SUCCESSFUL** — the real gate for the build.gradle signing-config change and the `DebugStrictMode` code.
- markdownlint (CI-pinned) clean on the new / edited docs.

## Maintainer / on-device closeout (deferred to the RC upload + v2.0.9)

These are inherently on-device / maintainer steps and are **not** done by this release (there is no device in the build environment):

- Upload the AAB to the Play Console **internal / closed testing track**.
- Sign with the **real upload keystore** (inject `keystore.properties` / `RUSTYNES_UPLOAD_*`); confirm Play App Signing enrollment.
- Publish the staged store listing via fastlane / the Play Console.
- Run the **crash-free-rate + ANR gate on real hardware** (Android vitals: crash rate < 1.09%, ANR < 0.47%, per-device < 8%).
- Verify the **monetization runtime** live on-device (AppLovin MAX + RevenueCat with real keys / products) — it remains behaviourally dormant until then.
- **Deferred per-feature gate migration (do NOT do on host):** the existing Play-Billing `LicenseManager` freemium gates (save-states / fast-forward / …) are intentionally **left as-is**; rewiring them to the monetization `feature_enabled` façade is a product-architecture decision needing on-device runtime validation, deferred to **v2.0.9**.
- Regenerate the Baseline / Startup Profile on-device against the v2.0.0 core (v2.0.9).

## What's next — the rest of "Harbor"

- **v2.0.5–v2.0.8** — iOS finalization on the v2.0.0 core.
- **v2.0.9** — joint on-device readiness verification for both apps (Baseline Profile capture, vitals, foldable WYSIWYG, live monetization runtime, the per-feature gate migration decision).
- **v2.1.0** — the **joint Google Play + Apple App Store + AltStore PAL + F-Droid launch**.

Full detail: `CHANGELOG.md` `[2.0.4]` · `docs/STATUS.md` (single source of truth) · `to-dos/plans/v2.0.4-android-rc-plan.md` · `to-dos/plans/v2.0.x-mobile-finalization-plan.md`.
